text:
King's College Hospital 

Department of Neurosciences 

Clinic Date: 23 December 2019 

Dr Priya Menon
Consultant Neurologist  
King's College Hospital  
Department of Neurosciences, King's College Hospital, London SE5 9RS  

Dear Dr Priya Menon
Consultant Neurologist,  

Samuel O’Connor, DOB: 14-03-1981, Hospital No: K913452 NHS No. 2478913650  
18 Willow Crescent, London, SE15 2QR

Thank you for asking me to review your patient following his recent discharge. He attended today with his wife. He described a steady routine since returning home and reports that he commutes mainly by bicycle, which he finds helpful for structure and general wellbeing. 

He explained that since discharge from hospital he has experienced two - three seizures, the last one being on 22-Oct. He is keeping a brief diary and notes that the events are typically followed by a variable recovery period with fatigue and reduced concentration for the rest of the day. There has been no clear precipitating factor identified by him. Night-time events have not been observed by his wife recently, and there has been no associated injury reported. 

On review of systems today, there were no clear focal neurological complaints volunteered. He did not describe a consistent warning or aura prior to the events. Examination in clinic was unremarkable and he was oriented with normal speech and language. 

We discussed the importance of ongoing documentation of events to help build a clearer picture, including any contextual factors around timing and recovery. He is comfortable to continue with routine follow-up. I will liaise with our EEG team regarding the feasibility of ambulatory monitoring if the frequency changes, and I will review him again in clinic in due course. 

Please let me know if there are new concerns or if further information becomes available from community records. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: since discharge from hospital he has experienced two - three seizures
He explained that since discharge from hospital he has experienced two - three seizures, the last one being on 22-Oct

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter states: "since discharge from hospital he has experienced two - three seizures, the last one being on 22-Oct." There is no explicit time interval given for "since discharge" relative to the clinic date (23 December 2019), so we cannot compute a rate per day/week/month/year. It confirms seizures occurred but does not provide a determinable frequency over a defined period. Therefore, per instructions, if seizures are mentioned but specific frequency cannot be determined, return "unknown."
